title: rosa glauca var. genuina
intro: Rosa glauca var. genuina, also known as the wild rose or dog rose, is a perennial shrub native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its clusters of small, fragrant flowers and thorny stems. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. Rosa glauca var. genuina is also known for its health benefits, including its ability to boost the immune system, improve cardiovascular health, and provide antioxidant protection. The plant can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal preparations such as infusions, teas, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
